name: abs
description: Returns the absolute value of an `INTEGER` or `FLOAT`.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A numeric value from which the absolute number will be returned.

name: acos
description: Returns the arccosine of a `FLOAT` in radians.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: An angle in radians.

name: all
description: Returns true if the predicate holds for all elements in the given `LIST<ANY>`.
signature: all(variable :: VARIABLE IN list :: LIST<ANY> WHERE predicate :: ANY) :: BOOLEAN

argumentDescription.name: variable
argumentDescription.description: A variable that can be used within the `WHERE` clause.

argumentDescription.name: list
argumentDescription.description: A predicate must hold for all elements in this list for the function to return `true`.

name: allReduce
description: Evaluates `expression` against each element of `LIST<ANY>`, with the result stored in `accumulator`. Returns true if `predicate` holds for each iteration of `accumulator`.
signature: allReduce(accumulator :: VARIABLE = initial :: ANY, variable :: VARIABLE IN list :: LIST<ANY> | reducer :: ANY, predicate :: BOOLEAN) :: BOOLEAN

argumentDescription.name: accumulator = initial
argumentDescription.description: The variable holding the result of reducer for each iteration, set to the value initial.

argumentDescription.name: variable IN list | reducer
argumentDescription.description: The iteration over a list that binds each list element to var and evaluates reducer to update the accumulator.

argumentDescription.name: predicate
argumentDescription.description: The predicate applied to the accumulator value for each element in the list.

name: any
description: Returns true if the predicate holds for at least one element in the given `LIST<ANY>`.
signature: any(variable :: VARIABLE IN list :: LIST<ANY> WHERE predicate :: ANY) :: BOOLEAN

argumentDescription.name: variable
argumentDescription.description: A variable that can be used within the `WHERE` clause.

argumentDescription.name: list
argumentDescription.description: A list for the predicate to check against.

name: asin
description: Returns the arcsine of a `FLOAT` in radians.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: An angle in radians.

name: atan
description: Returns the arctangent of a `FLOAT` in radians.

name: atan2
description: Returns the arctangent2 of a set of coordinates in radians.

argumentDescription.name: y
argumentDescription.description: A y angle in radians.

argumentDescription.name: x
argumentDescription.description: An x angle in radians.

name: avg

signature: avg(input :: INTEGER | FLOAT | DURATION) :: INTEGER | FLOAT | DURATION

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value aggregated to form an average.

name: btrim
description: Returns the given `STRING` with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value from which all leading and trailing whitespace will be removed.

name: btrim
description: Returns the given `STRING` with leading and trailing `trimCharacterString` characters removed.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value from which the leading and trailing trim character will be removed.

argumentDescription.name: trimCharacterString
argumentDescription.description: A character to be removed from the start and end of the given string.

argumentDescription.name: input

description: Returns the number of Unicode characters in a `STRING`.

description: Returns a list flattened to ....

name: date
description: Creates a `DATE` instant.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: Either a string representation of a temporal value, a map containing the single key 'timezone', or a map containing temporal values ('date', 'year', 'month', 'day', 'week', 'dayOfWeek', 'quarter', 'dayOfQuarter', 'ordinalDay') as components.

name: date
description: Creates a `DATE` instant.
signature: date(input = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: ANY, pattern = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: STRING) :: DATE

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: Either a string representation of a temporal value, a map containing the single key 'timezone', or a map containing temporal values ('date', 'year', 'month', 'day', 'week', 'dayOfWeek', 'quarter', 'dayOfQuarter', 'ordinalDay') as components.

argumentDescription.name: pattern
argumentDescription.description: A pattern used to parse a date value. If the pattern is not provided the value will be parsed according to default patterns.

name: date.realtime
description: Returns the current `DATE` instant using the realtime clock.

argumentDescription.name: timezone
argumentDescription.description: A string value representing a time zone.

name: date.statement
description: Returns the current `DATE` instant using the statement clock.

argumentDescription.name: timezone
argumentDescription.description: A string value representing a time zone.

name: date.transaction
description: Returns the current `DATE` instant using the transaction clock.

argumentDescription.name: timezone
argumentDescription.description: A string value representing a time zone.

name: date.truncate
description: Truncates the given temporal value to a `DATE` instant using the specified unit.
signature: date.truncate(unit :: STRING, input = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: ANY, fields = null :: MAP) :: DATE

argumentDescription.name: unit
argumentDescription.description: A string representing one of the following: 'day', 'week', 'month', 'weekYear', 'quarter', 'year', 'decade', 'century', 'millennium'.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: The date to be truncated using either `ZONED DATETIME`, `LOCAL DATETIME`, or `DATE`.

argumentDescription.name: fields
argumentDescription.description: A list of time components smaller than those specified in `unit` to preserve during truncation.

name: datetime
description: Creates a `ZONED DATETIME` instant.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: Either a string representation of a temporal value, a map containing the single key 'timezone', or a map containing temporal values ('year', 'month', 'day', 'hour', 'minute', 'second', 'millisecond', 'microsecond', 'nanosecond', 'timezone', 'epochSeconds', 'epochMillis') as components.

name: datetime

signature: datetime(input = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: ANY, pattern = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: STRING) :: ZONED DATETIME

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: Either a string representation of a temporal value, a map containing the single key 'timezone', or a map containing temporal values ('year', 'month', 'day', 'hour', 'minute', 'second', 'millisecond', 'microsecond', 'nanosecond', 'timezone', 'epochSeconds', 'epochMillis') as components.

argumentDescription.name: pattern
argumentDescription.description: A pattern used to parse a zoned datetime value. If the pattern is not provided the value will be parsed according to default patterns.

name: datetime.fromepoch
description: Creates a `ZONED DATETIME` given the seconds and nanoseconds since the start of the epoch.
signature: datetime.fromepoch(seconds :: INTEGER | FLOAT, nanoseconds :: INTEGER | FLOAT) :: ZONED DATETIME

argumentDescription.name: seconds
argumentDescription.description: The number of seconds from the UNIX epoch in the UTC time zone.

argumentDescription.name: nanoseconds
argumentDescription.description: The number of nanoseconds from the UNIX epoch in the UTC time zone. This can be added to seconds.

name: datetime.fromepochmillis
description: Creates a `ZONED DATETIME` given the milliseconds since the start of the epoch.

argumentDescription.name: milliseconds
argumentDescription.description: The number of milliseconds from the UNIX epoch in the UTC time zone.

argumentDescription.name: unit
argumentDescription.description: A string representing one of the following: 'microsecond', 'millisecond', 'second', 'minute', 'hour', 'day', 'week', 'month', 'weekYear', 'quarter', 'year', 'decade', 'century', 'millennium'.

argumentDescription.description: The date to be truncated using either `ZONED DATETIME`, `LOCAL DATETIME`, or `DATE`.

name: duration.between
description: Computes the `DURATION` between the `from` instant (inclusive) and the `to` instant (exclusive) in logical units.

argumentDescription.description: A temporal instant type (`DATE`, `LOCAL TIME`, `ZONED TIME`, `LOCAL DATETIME`, `ZONED DATETIME`) representing the starting instant.

argumentDescription.name: to
argumentDescription.description: A temporal instant type (`DATE`, `LOCAL TIME`, `ZONED TIME`, `LOCAL DATETIME`, `ZONED DATETIME`) representing the ending instant.

argumentDescription.name: to

name: format

name: pi
description: Returns the mathematical constant pi.

name: point
description: Returns a 2D or 3D point object, given two or respectively three coordinate values in the Cartesian coordinate system or WGS 84 geographic coordinate system.

argumentDescription.name: input

name: point.distance
description: Returns a `FLOAT` representing the distance between any two points in the same CRS. If the points are in the WGS 84 CRS, the function returns the geodesic distance (i.e., the shortest path along the curved surface of the Earth). If the points are in a Cartesian CRS, the function returns the Euclidean distance (i.e., the shortest straight-line distance in a flat, planar space).

argumentDescription.name: from
argumentDescription.description: A start point.

argumentDescription.name: to
argumentDescription.description: An end point in the same CRS as the start point.

name: point.withinBBox
description: Returns true if the provided point is within the bounding box defined by the two provided points.
signature: point.withinBBox(point :: POINT, lowerLeft :: POINT, upperRight :: POINT) :: BOOLEAN

argumentDescription.name: point
argumentDescription.description: A point to be confirmed in the bounding box.

argumentDescription.name: lowerLeft
argumentDescription.description: The lower left side point of the bounding box.

argumentDescription.name: upperRight
argumentDescription.description: The upper right side point of the bounding box.

name: properties

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: An entity to return the properties from.

name: reduce

argumentDescription.name: accumulator

argumentDescription.name: variable

name: replace
description: Returns a `STRING` in which all occurrences of a specified search `STRING` in the given `STRING` have been replaced by another (specified) replacement `STRING`.
signature: replace(original :: STRING, search :: STRING, replace :: STRING) :: STRING

argumentDescription.name: original
argumentDescription.description: The string to be modified.

argumentDescription.name: search
argumentDescription.description: The value to replace in the original string.

argumentDescription.name: replace
argumentDescription.description: The value to be inserted in the original string.

name: replace
description: Returns a `STRING` in which all occurrences of a specified search `STRING` in the given `STRING` have been replaced by another (specified) replacement `STRING`.
signature: replace(original :: STRING, search :: STRING, replace :: STRING, limit :: INTEGER) :: STRING

argumentDescription.name: search
argumentDescription.description: The value to replace in the original string.

argumentDescription.name: replace
argumentDescription.description: The value to be inserted in the original string.

argumentDescription.name: limit
argumentDescription.description: The maximum amount of times the search value should be replaced in the string, starting from the left.

argumentDescription.name: precision

argumentDescription.name: trimCharacterString

name: time
description: Creates a `ZONED TIME` instant.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: Either a string representation of a temporal value, a map containing the single key 'timezone', or a map containing temporal values ('hour', 'minute', 'second', 'millisecond', 'microsecond', 'nanosecond', 'timezone') as components.

name: time
description: Creates a `ZONED TIME` instant.
signature: time(input = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: ANY, pattern = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: STRING) :: ZONED TIME

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: Either a string representation of a temporal value, a map containing the single key 'timezone', or a map containing temporal values ('hour', 'minute', 'second', 'millisecond', 'microsecond', 'nanosecond', 'timezone') as components.

argumentDescription.name: pattern
argumentDescription.description: A pattern used to parse a zoned time value. If the pattern is not provided the value will be parsed according to default patterns.

name: time.realtime
description: Returns the current `ZONED TIME` instant using the realtime clock.

argumentDescription.name: timezone
argumentDescription.description: A string value representing a time zone.

name: time.statement
description: Returns the current `ZONED TIME` instant using the statement clock.

argumentDescription.name: timezone
argumentDescription.description: A string value representing a time zone.

name: time.transaction
description: Returns the current `ZONED TIME` instant using the transaction clock.

argumentDescription.description: A string value representing a time zone.

name: time.truncate
description: Truncates the given temporal value to a `ZONED TIME` instant using the specified unit.
signature: time.truncate(unit :: STRING, input = DEFAULT_TEMPORAL_ARGUMENT :: ANY, fields = null :: MAP) :: ZONED TIME

argumentDescription.name: unit
argumentDescription.description: A string representing one of the following: 'microsecond', 'millisecond', 'second', 'minute', 'hour', 'day'.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: The date to be truncated using either `ZONED DATETIME`, `LOCAL DATETIME`, `ZONED TIME`, or `LOCAL TIME`.

argumentDescription.name: fields
argumentDescription.description: A list of time components smaller than those specified in `unit` to preserve during truncation.

name: timestamp
description: Returns the difference, measured in milliseconds, between the current time and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC

name: toBoolean
description: Converts a `BOOLEAN`, `STRING` or `INTEGER` value to a `BOOLEAN` value. For `INTEGER` values, 0 is defined to be false and any other `INTEGER` is defined to be true.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into a boolean.

name: toBooleanList
description: Converts a `LIST<ANY>` of values to a `LIST<BOOLEAN>` values. If any values are not convertible to `BOOLEAN` they will be null in the `LIST<BOOLEAN>` returned.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A list of values to be converted into a list of booleans.

name: toBooleanOrNull
description: Converts a value to a `BOOLEAN` value, or null if the value cannot be converted.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into a boolean or null.

name: toFloat
description: Converts a `STRING`, `INTEGER` or `FLOAT` value to a `FLOAT` value.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into a float.

name: toFloatList
description: Converts a `LIST<ANY>` to a `LIST<FLOAT>` values. If any values are not convertible to `FLOAT` they will be null in the `LIST<FLOAT>` returned.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A list of values to be converted into a list of floats.

name: toFloatList
description: Converts a `LIST<ANY> | VECTOR` to a `LIST<FLOAT>` values. If any values are not convertible to `FLOAT` they will be null in the `LIST<FLOAT>` returned.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A list of values or vector to be converted into a list of floats.

name: toFloatOrNull
description: Converts a value to a `FLOAT` value, or null if the value cannot be converted.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into a float or null.

name: toInteger
description: Converts a `BOOLEAN`, `STRING`, `INTEGER` or `FLOAT` value to an `INTEGER` value. For `BOOLEAN` values, true is defined to be 1 and false is defined to be 0.
signature: toInteger(input :: BOOLEAN | STRING | INTEGER | FLOAT) :: INTEGER

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into an integer.

name: toIntegerList
description: Converts a `LIST<ANY>` to a `LIST<INTEGER>` values. If any values are not convertible to `INTEGER` they will be null in the `LIST<INTEGER>` returned.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A list of values to be converted into a list of integers.

name: toIntegerList
description: Converts a `LIST<ANY> | VECTOR` to a `LIST<INTEGER>` values. If any values are not convertible to `INTEGER` they will be null in the `LIST<INTEGER>` returned.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A list of values or vector to be converted into a list of integers.

name: toIntegerOrNull
description: Converts a value to an `INTEGER` value, or null if the value cannot be converted.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into an integer or null.

name: toLower

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A string to be converted into lowercase.

name: toString

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into a string.

name: toStringList
description: Converts a `LIST<ANY>` to a `LIST<STRING>` values. If any values are not convertible to `STRING` they will be null in the `LIST<STRING>` returned.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A list of values to be converted into a list of strings.

name: toStringOrNull
description: Converts an `INTEGER`, `FLOAT`, `BOOLEAN`, `POINT` or temporal type (i.e. `DATE`, `ZONED TIME`, `LOCAL TIME`, `ZONED DATETIME`, `LOCAL DATETIME` or `DURATION`) value to a `STRING`, or null if the value cannot be converted.

argumentDescription.name: input
argumentDescription.description: A value to be converted into a string or null.

name: toUpper
description: Returns the given `STRING` in uppercase.

argumentDescription.name: input

signature: trim([[LEADING | TRAILING | BOTH] [trimCharacterString :: STRING] FROM] input :: STRING) :: STRING

description: Returns a `STRING` representation of the `RELATIONSHIP` type.

name: upper
description: Returns the given `STRING` in uppercase.

name: vector

argumentDescription.description: The type of each coordinate in the vector.

name: vector.similarity.cosine
description: Returns a `FLOAT` representing the similarity between the argument vectors based on their cosine.

argumentDescription.name: a
argumentDescription.description: A list representing the first vector.

argumentDescription.name: b
argumentDescription.description: A list representing the second vector.

name: vector.similarity.cosine
description: Returns a `FLOAT` representing the similarity between the argument vectors based on their cosine.
signature: vector.similarity.cosine(a :: VECTOR | LIST<INTEGER | FLOAT>, b :: VECTOR | LIST<INTEGER | FLOAT>) :: FLOAT

argumentDescription.name: a
argumentDescription.description: A vector or list value representing the first vector.

argumentDescription.name: b
argumentDescription.description: A vector or list value representing the second vector.

name: vector.similarity.euclidean
description: Returns a `FLOAT` representing the similarity between the argument vectors based on their Euclidean distance.
signature: vector.similarity.euclidean(a :: LIST<INTEGER | FLOAT>, b :: LIST<INTEGER | FLOAT>) :: FLOAT

argumentDescription.name: a
argumentDescription.description: A list representing the first vector.

argumentDescription.name: b
argumentDescription.description: A list representing the second vector.

name: vector.similarity.euclidean
description: Returns a `FLOAT` representing the similarity between the argument vectors based on their Euclidean distance.

name: vector_distance
description: Returns a `FLOAT` representing the distance between the two vector values based on the selected `vectorDistanceMetric` algorithm.

argumentDescription.description: The first vector.

argumentDescription.name: vector2
argumentDescription.description: The second vector.

argumentDescription.name: vectorDistanceMetric
argumentDescription.description: The vector distance algorithm to calculate the distance by.

name: vector_norm
description: Returns a `FLOAT` representing the distance between the given vector and an origin vector, which is a vector with the same dimension with all coordinates set to zero, calculated using the specified `vectorDistanceMetric`.
signature: vector_norm(vector1 :: VECTOR, vector2 :: VECTOR, coordinateType :: [EUCLIDEAN, EUCLIDEAN_SQUARED, MANHATTAN, COSINE, DOT, HAMMING]) :: FLOAT

argumentDescription.name: vector
argumentDescription.description: A vector for which the norm to the origin vector will be computed.

argumentDescription.name: vectorDistanceMetric
argumentDescription.description: The vector distance algorithm to calculate the distance by.

name: zoned_time
